215693.explanation: The municipality scores poorly on public toilet facilities. Meanwhile, every toilet counts. Nearly 1 in 4 people stay home fearing they cannot find a toilet in time. Lokaal Maashorst wants everyone to be able to go to the toilet on time, even outside the home.

215694.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst finds sport important for the development and health of every child. If parents cannot afford this, the municipality should assist.

215695.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst recognizes that carnival is a large and important part of our culture. Carnival halls have traditionally been municipally owned and have been made available to local associations for a long time. This tradition is valuable and we must continue it.

215696.explanation: In our water-rich country, it is important that we are able to survive in and around water from a young age. Lokaal Maashorst therefore considers learning to swim and swim safety to be of great importance. School swimming is also more than just obtaining a swimming diploma. It fosters connection.

215697.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst believes it is important that every community has basic facilities. However, that does not mean that every community should get a pop music venue. That is too expensive. Moreover, you end up competing against each other excessively. We prefer to stimulate the pop venues that already exist, so they become accessible to everyone.

215698.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst believes it is important to achieve one high standard for all our school buildings. Some school buildings are already good enough or were just completed. Therefore, it is not necessary to invest money in ALL buildings.

215699.explanation: There are already several large multifunctional sports halls in the municipality of Maashorst.

215700.explanation: Associations are vital to Lokaal Maashorst. That is why we support our associations when they are in difficulty.

215701.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst encourages our sports associations. We do this through targeted investments where there is need and necessity.

215702.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst advocates for a professional fireworks show organized by the municipality. This offers a safe and attractive alternative to private fireworks, with less nuisance and damage, less pressure on emergency services, and a shared, festive New Year’s celebration.

215703.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst believes that enforcement officers in our municipality should be more visible to improve the overall sense of safety.

215704.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst believes that there should be more camera surveillance in public places in the municipality to improve the overall sense of safety.

215705.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst wants to strongly improve bicycle safety in Maashorst. Fatbikes often cause nuisance and dangerous situations. Therefore, fatbikes will be banned in shopping centers and more frequently checked for, for example, illegal modifications.

215706.explanation: Municipal enforcement officers are exposed to threatening situations. The number of violent incidents is high. An enforcement officer should be able to protect themselves in these dangerous situations through self-defense. However, this should be implemented as a pilot.

215707.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst believes it is important that our own residents get priority for housing. This is so that more residents can continue living in their own neighborhood and thus remain involved in their own environment.

215710.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst ensures affordability and sufficient housing supply in every community, with attention to flow-through and diversity. We pursue active land policy by more frequently purchasing land, so the municipality maintains control over speed and affordability.

215711.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst finds it important that homes are built for our residents, not for expensive rental. Especially when it comes to our cheap and affordable housing. Therefore, we support an owner-occupancy obligation for these homes.

215712.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst believes that the municipality should first build within the built-up area before expanding outside of it. If housing construction within the core is not possible, building outside the core is necessary so our residents can continue living in our cores.

215713.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst considers it important that everyone in our municipality can participate. Self-reliance is important, but if that is not yet possible, the municipality must help residents.

215714.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst considers it important that everyone in our municipality can participate. However, we are not in favor of obligations, as these tend to cause more problems than solutions. We would rather stimulate socially meaningful day activities than mandate them.

215715.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst considers it important that everyone in our municipality can participate. Subsidized jobs for the long-term unemployed are, however, pointless. Lokaal Maashorst would rather encourage residents toward independent and paid employment. This increases self-reliance.

215716.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst sees that the population composition is changing. More and more people are informal caregivers. In our municipality, we help each other and stand ready for each other. This applies not only to social assistance recipients but also increasingly to those who are employed. Therefore, we oppose exemption from job application requirements.

215718.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst believes it is important that the municipality assists residents who cannot afford energy-saving measures themselves. This is to prevent people from being left in the cold.

215720.explanation: The Market in Uden used to be a lively village center. We would like to bring that back. There are plenty of options to compensate for the number of freed-up parking spaces nearby.

215721.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst sees that a train station has been promised to our residents continuously for twenty years. While for twenty years, again and again, expensive reports have proven that it is not feasible. It is not an honest story. We do our municipality a great disservice this way.

215723.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst sees that the municipality already spends 1.5 million euros per year on advertising within the municipality. We think this is too much. Our residents are often already informed about the benefits of shared transport in other ways, for example by the provider itself. They already earn enough from this.

215724.explanation: Parking in and near the shopping center of Uden is important to keep our center accessible.

215725.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst observes that parking fees have risen sharply recently. We consider this an undesirable development. Lokaal Maashorst opposes the continuous increase in parking fees and taxes without clarity on where the money goes.

215726.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst wants to strongly improve bicycle safety within our municipality. We want the fast cycling paths in Uden to be not only conveniently located but also attractively situated. This encourages bicycle use.

215727.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst wants to invest in public transport to make it more attractive for residents to leave their cars behind. It is also important for all ages, young and old, that there is good public transport to important facilities.

215728.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst thinks it is important to tailor speed limits according to what is best suited for each location. On some roads, driving too slowly can cause dangerous traffic situations.

215729.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst wants us to realize suitable housing for all our target groups. People with care needs are also part of this.

215730.explanation: Lokaal Maashorst stimulates local grassroots initiatives.

215669.explanation: We as BBB stand for personal contact with residents. We choose to be close and clear.

215670.explanation: BBB Maashorst supports citizen participation and stays close to residents. We do not see randomly chosen representation through lottery as the best choice because this also excludes some residents.

215671.explanation: BBB Maashorst advocates for short lines of communication and adequate decision-making. A formal complaints procedure can sometimes take months and causes more frustration among residents. An independent municipal ombudsman could speed up this process.

215672.explanation: We as BBB choose short lines between policy and practice, where effective regulation is leading.

215674.explanation: BBB Maashorst does not want to make the municipality of Maashorst a tourist hotspot. Tourism is nice, but daily life should take precedence. We do not see investing in advertising and promotion as a priority for a municipality.

215675.explanation: BBB Maashorst supports economic development and space for entrepreneurs. BBB Maashorst sets the condition that the focus should mainly be on local businesses and that large multinationals and/or investment companies should not dominate. Additionally, there are conditions regarding accessibility and nuisance that should not pose obstacles for residents in the nearby villages.

215677.explanation: BBB Maashorst sees this as symbolic politics and wants to focus on measures that are realistic and genuinely useful.

215678.explanation: BBB Maashorst supports entrepreneurship and creating space for it, but we believe there should be more room in spatial plans to make it easier to operate a business from home.

215679.explanation: BBB Maashorst stands for local interests. We believe local entrepreneurs deserve the first chance, and large distribution centers do not belong in that, especially if natural areas or agricultural land are sacrificed.

215680.explanation: BBB Maashorst stands for the interests of local residents and sees a priority rule for housing as conflicting with the principle of equality in this time of high housing shortage.

215681.explanation: BBB Maashorst stands for integration through participation. Learning the Dutch language is an inseparable part of this.

215682.explanation: BBB Maashorst wants to limit the reception of asylum seekers in the municipality as much as possible. This is because facilities such as security agencies, education, healthcare, and housing are insufficient. We also advocate conducting a support survey in a specific neighborhood before setting up an asylum seekers' center (AZC). Furthermore, BBB Maashorst believes that an AZC does not belong on agricultural or nature grounds.

215685.explanation: BBB Maashorst stands for neighborly solidarity and supports local citizen initiatives as long as they are simple and small-scale.

215686.explanation: BBB chooses to plant a new tree for every tree that is cut down.

215688.explanation: BBB Maashorst stands for neighborliness and connection. Such a competition makes residents vital and brings them together.

215689.explanation: BBB Maashorst supports citizen initiatives and wants to realize them together with residents. This kind of policy makes it unnecessarily difficult and hard to justify.

215690.explanation: BBB Maashorst is against such a ban. However, it is necessary to provide additional education on how to burn according to the Swiss method.

215691.explanation: BBB Maashorst believes that solar parks and wind turbines do not belong in rural areas. We prefer solar panels on roofs.

215692.explanation: BBB Maashorst opposes a general ban on the expansion of intensive livestock farming. Such a ban threatens farmers, food security, and the livability of rural areas. Moreover, a local ban does not solve the problem but shifts production to countries with lower environmental standards. This does not reduce the problem but merely relocates it.

215693.explanation: BBB Maashorst supports facilities close to residents. Therefore, adequate facilities for hygienic work are encouraged by BBB.

215694.explanation: BBB Maashorst supports this statement. It promotes equal opportunities for less privileged residents and fosters neighborly solidarity, provided proper oversight is in place.

215695.explanation: BBB opts for less administrative burden. After all, there are enough barns empty at farmers.

215696.explanation: BBB Maashorst believes that school swimming belongs to the basic skills of children.

215697.explanation: BBB listens to the needs of residents. If there is demand from a village center, we facilitate it.

215698.explanation: BBB Maashorst sees this as an important investment. Educational facilities are essential for good and quality education.

215699.explanation: BBB Maashorst stands for maintaining or expanding local facilities in every village center.

215700.explanation: BBB Maashorst supports local associations and promotes neighborly solidarity, choosing local customization.

215701.explanation: BBB Maashorst supports local associations and promotes neighborly solidarity, choosing local customization.

215702.explanation: BBB chooses to have a separate fireworks show for every village center.

215703.explanation: BBB Maashorst opts for a firm approach to nuisance and increasing safety in the municipality. Extra capacity is needed for this. More municipal enforcement officers (boa’s) can be a valuable addition to the limited police capacity. Boa’s in domain I focus mainly on livability and public order, such as tackling nuisance, illegal parking, and minor annoyances in public spaces. Boa’s in domain II supervise environmental, nature, and rural areas, for example dealing with illegal dumping and violations in nature and agricultural zones. With extra enforcement officers in these domains, Maashorst can act more targeted and effectively.

215704.explanation: BBB Maashorst does not support additional camera surveillance. This is already present to some extent. This investigative tool does not always lead to good results or usable evidence.

215705.explanation: BBB Maashorst does not choose this. The problem is not the fatbikes themselves but the troublemakers who operate them.

215706.explanation: BBB Maashorst stands for neighborly solidarity, professionalism, and the golden rule. Equipping boa’s with a baton adds no value because boa’s are only allowed to use force in self-defense and must be restrained in using their authority granted by the Police Act for actions such as making arrests. According to BBB, boa’s should focus more on neighborhood-oriented work and stay close to residents.

215707.explanation: BBB Maashorst chooses the local residents and neighborly solidarity and therefore supports this statement.

215710.explanation: BBB Maashorst stands for professionalism and the golden rule. This is seen as unfair competition, hindering free market operation.

215711.explanation: BBB Maashorst supports this statement and sees it as a necessary rule to contribute to solving the housing shortage.

215712.explanation: BBB Maashorst focuses on preserving the countryside and does not see housing construction on agricultural land as an option.

215713.explanation: BBB Maashorst stands for care and support close to the residents. Therefore, we find it important that residents are well-informed and guided when using poverty reduction schemes, ensuring these provisions are genuinely accessible to everyone who needs them.

215714.explanation: BBB Maashorst opts for caring for each other through mutual support (neighborliness). A social contribution is a repayment of taxpayers’ money to the local community.

215715.explanation: BBB Maashorst does not support such a subsidy. These schemes lead to extra bureaucracy and unnecessary complications. Additionally, monitoring these schemes results in expanding the civil service, which increases municipal expenses.

215716.explanation: BBB Maashorst disagrees with this statement. After all, there are also working people who are informal caregivers and have to fulfill employer obligations. This would create inequality.

215718.explanation: BBB Maashorst follows the principle: those who are not registered green cannot act green.

215720.explanation: BBB Maashorst believes the market in Uden must remain accessible even for people with limited mobility.

215721.explanation: BBB Maashorst stands for better mobility facilities in every village center.

215723.explanation: BBB Maashorst sees this as patronizing and not a necessary investment.

215724.explanation: BBB Maashorst believes there are enough parking spaces in Uden.

215725.explanation: BBB Maashorst supports this statement. This is to better distribute mobility flows and to be able to finance other initiatives.

215726.explanation: BBB Maashorst supports better mobility facilities and would like to better understand whether there is also a need for this in a village center.

215727.explanation: BBB Maashorst stands for neighborliness in which residents take care of each other.

215728.explanation: BBB Maashorst stands for better traffic flow without obstructive restrictions, such as speed bumps.

215729.explanation: BBB Maashorst supports good care for people with psychological issues. However, temporary housing in rural areas is not desirable.

215730.explanation: BBB Maashorst disagrees with this statement because it will lead to unnecessarily many regulations and bureaucracy.

statements.moreInfo: A citizen assembly consists of residents selected by lottery to discuss an important topic. They provide advice to the municipality. A citizen assembly ensures broader involvement: residents can calmly and well-informed make recommendations together. On the other hand, a citizen assembly costs time and money, and the municipal council—regardless of the advice—always makes its own decision.

shootoutStatements.title: In all population centers of Maashorst the maximum speed limit within built-up areas should be 30 km/h.
Residents of Maashorst should be able to take complaints about the municipality to a municipal ombudsman.
The municipality should once again provide funding for school swimming.
The municipality should oppose the establishment of large commercial buildings such as distribution centers.
The municipality should invest money in building temporary storage sheds for carnival associations.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.
The municipality should oppose new development outside the built-up area.
The municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) in Maashorst should be given batons.
Children should receive a small payment from the municipality for picking up litter.
The municipality of Maashorst should ban the use of wood-burning stoves.
There should be subsidized jobs for the long-term unemployed in our municipality.
The municipality should allocate more funds for public toilets in shopping centers and at tourist attractions.
The municipality should ensure that migrant workers learn Dutch if they want to stay in Maashorst long-term.
Asylum seekers who are allowed to stay in the Netherlands should be given priority when allocating social housing.
The municipality should ban advertising for fossil-fuel products in public spaces.
The municipality should pay sports-club memberships for more children if their parents or caregivers have little money.
The municipality should organize its own fireworks display on New Year's Eve.
The Market in Uden should be made car-free.
The municipality should invest more in improving all school buildings.
Paid parking on busy shopping streets should be made more expensive.
The municipality should provide extra funding to the sports clubs in Maashorst.
Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
More people should be able to access programs that provide help for poverty.
The municipality should continue to advocate for a train connection to Uden.
Maashorst should build fast bike connections between all population centres in the municipality.
The municipality should invest in tailored transport between the main centre and surrounding areas (for example, a dial-a-ride bus).
